architecture.summary: MARD is a parameter-efficient reasoning distillation framework that adds lightweight adapter modules to specific Transformer components (feed-forward network projections and self-attention output projections) of a student model while freezing backbone parameters. A strong teacher model provides chain-of-thought reasoning trajectories offline. The student learns to replicate teacher reasoning through focused distillation at identified bottleneck modules, with adaptive supervision that weights reasoning losses by problem difficulty.
trained_components: adapter modules at self-attention output projections

biblio.title: MARD: Module-Aware Reasoning Distillation for Language Models with Adaptive Supervision

claims.statement: Different Transformer modules contribute unequally to reasoning, and feed-forward network projections and the output projection of self-attention are primary bottlenecks for reasoning.
The approach adopts an offline distillation setting where a strong teacher model provides reasoning trajectories in advance.
An adaptive supervision strategy adjusts the strength of reasoning-related losses according to problem difficulty.
The approach demonstrates consistent improvements over strong baselines on mathematical reasoning benchmarks.
Ablation studies confirm the importance of both module-aware placement and adaptive supervision.
